_doc: Compiled from Framework v1.7 PART D (Quality Requirements) + PART I (Scientific Accuracy) + PART G (Language)

quality_requirements.medical_disclaimer: This microbiome analysis provides insights into gut bacterial composition and potential metabolic functions based on DNA sequencing. It is NOT a diagnostic test and does NOT diagnose, treat, cure, or prevent any disease. The findings suggest optimization opportunities and potential metabolic patterns, not medical conditions. All interpretations are probabilistic and functional assessments based on compositional data, not definitive clinical diagnoses. Always consult qualified healthcare providers for medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment decisions.
quality_requirements.gmwi2_explanation: GMWI2 is a disease-agnostic health indicator based on 155 bacterial species with health/disease associations, developed from 8,069 stool metagenomes across 26 countries using Lasso-penalized logistic regression.
quality_requirements.timeline_clarification: Gut bacterial composition changes can begin to manifest in stool within 3-7 days after sustained dietary pattern shift. However, meaningful improvements in guild abundances and health impacts typically require 6-12 weeks of consistent dietary and supplement intervention. Early compositional changes (days) precede functional ecosystem remodeling (weeks to months).
quality_requirements.stool_snapshot: Your test analyzes bacteria in your stool — a snapshot of what's leaving your system. It reflects your recent past (3-7 days), not what's happening right now.

clr_is_sample_relative.rule: CLR values describe competitive position relative to THIS sample's geometric mean, NOT absolute functional capacity
clr_is_sample_relative.correct_language: CLR -1.59 means competitively disadvantaged relative to sample mean
clr_is_sample_relative.key_point: A guild can have negative CLR yet maintain adequate function through high redundancy or efficient metabolism

abundance_function_nonlinear.rule: Guild functional output is NOT strictly proportional to abundance
abundance_function_nonlinear.correct_language: Below threshold; relationship between abundance and function non-linear

proteolytic_balanced_perspective.rule: Present both risks (excessive) AND benefits (moderate BCAA processing, nitrogen cycling)
proteolytic_balanced_perspective.correct_language: While excessive activity generates inflammatory metabolites, moderate activity necessary for BCAA metabolism
proteolytic_balanced_perspective.incorrect_language: Proteolytic organisms generate harmful metabolites (only negative framing)

substrate_flow_complexity.rule: Substrate flow inference has multiple confounders
confounders: Substrate specificity — not all fiber products feed all downstream guilds
Direct dietary sources — oligosaccharides can reach colon without Fiber Degrader processing
Spatial/temporal dynamics — fermentation staged along colon
Alternative mechanisms — low CLR may reflect efficient downstream clearance, not substrate limitation
substrate_flow_complexity.correct_language: May reduce availability; relationship complex due to confounders
substrate_flow_complexity.incorrect_language: Low Fiber Degraders causes low Bifidobacteria (oversimplified causation)

what_it_cannot_measure: Actual enzyme expression (gene presence ≠ gene activity)

client_facing_tone.framing: Optimization opportunities, not diseases. Possibilities, not diagnoses.

in_scope: What we see → what it means → why it happened
